message:
fix: remove duplicate functions and improve json_escape

Code review cleanup:
- Remove duplicate json_escape() at line 779 (incorrect printf with newline)
- Remove duplicate ntm_wait_completion() at line 6585
- Rename is_repo_completed → is_sweep_repo_completed (avoid collision with
  sync's is_repo_completed that uses SYNC_COMPLETED array)
- Add \b and \f escapes to json_escape() for completeness

Enhanced run_secret_scan():
- Add detect-secrets as layer 2 fallback
- Expand regex patterns with specific token formats (AWS, GitHub, Slack, etc)
- Use case-sensitive matching for token patterns
- Track which tool was used for better reporting

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update README install snippet to include cache-buster query param.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-05T09:36:32.391624693-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T09:40:57.125744226-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T09:40:57.125744226-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed: installer uses redirect fallback + cache-buster docs","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6d06","depends_on_id":"bd-szcn","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-05T09:36:32.408462829-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6kme","title":"Implement portable JSON parsing (json_get_field)","description":"# Portable JSON Parsing Implementation

## Parent Epic: bd-9o2h (NTM Driver Integration Layer)

## Purpose
Create json_get_field() function that works on systems without jq using layered fallbacks.

## Implementation

```bash
json_get_field() {
    local json="$1" field="$2"
    
    # Best: jq (most reliable)
    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        jq -r --arg f "$field" '.[$f] // empty' <<<"$json" 2>/dev/null
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Fallback: python3
    if command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then
        python3 -c "
import json,sys
field=sys.argv[1]
data=json.loads(sys.stdin.read())
v=data.get(field,'')
print(v if isinstance(v,(str,int,float,bool)) else json.dumps(v))
" "$field" <<<"$json" 2>/dev/null
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Fallback: perl with JSON::PP
    if command -v perl &>/dev/null && perl -MJSON::PP -e1 2>/dev/null; then
        perl -MJSON::PP -0777 -ne '...' "$field" <<<"$json" 2>/dev/null
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Last resort: minimal sed (flat strings only, fragile)
    sed -nE 's/.*"'"$field"'":[[:space:]]*"([^"]*)".*/\1/p' <<<"$json" | head -n1
}
```

## Also Implement
- json_is_success() - Check if JSON has success:true
- json_escape() - Escape string for JSON embedding (backslash, quotes, newlines, tabs)

## Testing
- Test with jq available
- Test with only python3
- Test with only perl
- Test with only sed (minimal fallback)
- Test nested objects (should return JSON for non-primitives)

## Considerations
- sed fallback is fragile, only works for simple flat strings
- Always try to use jq/python when available
- Document limitations in code comments","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:48:37.5200696-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:02:30.82913617-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:02:30.82913617-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented json_get_field(), json_is_success(), json_escape() with layered fallbacks (jq→python3→perl→sed). All 31 unit tests pass. Boolean false handling fixed in jq."}

-{"id":"bd-6nuc","title":"Implement per-repo configuration loading","description":"Implements load_repo_agent_config() to support per-repository agent-sweep customization.

## Background

The NTM integration plan specifies support for per-repo .ru-agent.yml or .ru-agent.json files that can override default agent-sweep behavior. This enables repository maintainers to:
- Enable/disable agent sweeps for their repo
- Customize file denylist patterns
- Set repo-specific size limits
- Configure custom phase prompts or skip certain phases
- Define pre/post hooks

## Function to Implement

### load_repo_agent_config()

Purpose: Load and merge per-repo configuration with global defaults.

Location: ~/.config/ru/agent-sweep.yml (global) merged with repo/.ru-agent.yml

Config Schema:
```yaml
# Enable/disable for this repo
enabled: true

# Override file patterns to deny
file_denylist_extra:
  - "*.generated.ts"
  - "vendor/*"

# Override file size limit (bytes)
max_file_size: 5242880  # 5MB

# Skip phases
skip_phases:
  - release  # Skip phase 3 for this repo

# Custom understanding context
extra_context: |
  This repo uses a monorepo structure.
  Database migrations are in db/migrations/.

# Hooks (run in repo context)
pre_sweep_hook: "./scripts/pre-agent.sh"
post_sweep_hook: "./scripts/post-agent.sh"
```

Implementation Steps:
1. Check for .ru-agent.yml or .ru-agent.json in repo root
2. Parse YAML/JSON using portable approach (yq -> python3 -> simple parser)
3. Merge with global config (repo overrides global)
4. Validate configuration values
5. Return merged config as associative array or temp file

## Portable YAML Parsing

Since this is pure Bash, implement layered fallback:
1. yq (if available) - fastest, most reliable
2. python3 -c "import yaml..." (common fallback)
3. Simple key: value parser for flat configs

## Error Handling

- Missing config file is NOT an error (use defaults)
- Invalid YAML/JSON should warn but not fail the sweep
- Unknown keys should warn but be ignored
- Validate types (numbers are numbers, booleans are booleans)

## Related Beads

- Parent epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)
- Used by: bd-kczb (cmd_agent_sweep main function)
- Used by: bd-b00c (run_single_agent_workflow)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Loads .ru-agent.yml from repo root if present
- [ ] Falls back to .ru-agent.json if YAML not found
- [ ] Merges repo config with global defaults correctly
- [ ] Unknown keys logged but do not cause failure
- [ ] Invalid config values produce clear warnings
- [ ] Works without yq installed (python3 fallback)
- [ ] Works without python3 installed (simple parser fallback)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:22:02.187186112-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:10:21.672950375-05:00"}

 {"id":"bd-6p3o","title":"Write comprehensive security guardrail tests","description":"Implements detailed tests for all security guardrails with extensive logging.

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Test File
scripts/test_security_guardrails.sh

## Purpose
Ensure security guardrails work correctly and cannot be bypassed. Every test must have detailed logging to verify exactly what was checked and why it passed/failed.

## Test Categories

### 1. File Denylist Tests

```bash
test_denylist_exact_matches() {
    log_test_start "Exact denylist matches"
    
    local denied_files=(
        ".env"
        ".env.local"
        ".env.production"
        "config/.env"
        "id_rsa"
        "id_ed25519"
        ".git/config"
        "node_modules/package/index.js"
        "secrets.json"
        "credentials.yaml"
    )
    
    for file in "${denied_files[@]}"; do
        log_verbose "Testing denylist for: $file"
        if \! is_file_denied "$file"; then
            log_error "FAIL: $file should be denied"
            return 1
        fi
        log_success "PASS: $file correctly denied"
    done
}

test_denylist_glob_patterns() {
    log_test_start "Glob pattern matching"
    
    # Test *.pem pattern
    is_file_denied "server.pem" || fail "*.pem should match server.pem"
    is_file_denied "certs/client.pem" || fail "*.pem should match nested"
    
    # Test **/node_modules/** pattern
    is_file_denied "frontend/node_modules/pkg/a.js" || fail "node_modules nested"
    
    # Test .git/** pattern
    is_file_denied ".git/objects/abc123" || fail ".git internals"
}

test_denylist_allowed_files() {
    log_test_start "Allowed file verification"
    
    local allowed_files=(
        "src/main.py"
        "README.md"
        "config/settings.yaml"  # Not secrets.yaml
        ".gitignore"
        "package.json"
    )
    
    for file in "${allowed_files[@]}"; do
        log_verbose "Testing allow for: $file"
        if is_file_denied "$file"; then
            log_error "FAIL: $file should be allowed but was denied"
            return 1
        fi
        log_success "PASS: $file correctly allowed"
    done
}
```

### 2. File Size Limit Tests

```bash
test_file_size_enforcement() {
    log_test_start "File size limit enforcement"
    
    local test_dir=$(mktemp -d)
    
    # Create files of various sizes
    dd if=/dev/zero of="$test_dir/small.bin" bs=1K count=100 2>/dev/null
    dd if=/dev/zero of="$test_dir/at_limit.bin" bs=1M count=10 2>/dev/null
    dd if=/dev/zero of="$test_dir/over_limit.bin" bs=1M count=11 2>/dev/null
    
    log_verbose "Testing 100KB file (should pass)"
    is_file_oversized "$test_dir/small.bin" 10485760 && fail "100KB should pass"
    
    log_verbose "Testing 10MB file at limit (should pass)"
    is_file_oversized "$test_dir/at_limit.bin" 10485760 && fail "At limit should pass"
    
    log_verbose "Testing 11MB file over limit (should fail)"
    is_file_oversized "$test_dir/over_limit.bin" 10485760 || fail "Over limit should fail"
    
    rm -rf "$test_dir"
    log_success "All size tests passed"
}
```

### 3. Binary Detection Tests

```bash
test_binary_detection() {
    log_test_start "Binary file detection"
    
    local test_dir=$(mktemp -d)
    
    # Create text file
    echo "Hello, world" > "$test_dir/text.txt"
    
    # Create binary file (has null bytes)
    printf "binary\x00content" > "$test_dir/binary.bin"
    
    # Create ELF header
    printf "\x7fELF" > "$test_dir/executable"
    
    log_verbose "Testing text file (should not be binary)"
    is_binary_file "$test_dir/text.txt" && fail "Text should not be binary"
    
    log_verbose "Testing binary file (should be binary)"
    is_binary_file "$test_dir/binary.bin" || fail "Null bytes should be binary"
    
    log_verbose "Testing ELF file (should be binary)"
    is_binary_file "$test_dir/executable" || fail "ELF should be binary"
    
    rm -rf "$test_dir"
    log_success "All binary detection tests passed"
}
```

### 4. Secret Scanning Tests

```bash
test_secret_scanning_gitleaks() {
    log_test_start "Secret scanning with gitleaks"
    
    # Skip if gitleaks not available
    if \! command -v gitleaks >/dev/null; then
        log_skip "gitleaks not installed"
        return 0
    fi
    
    local test_dir=$(mktemp -d)
    git init "$test_dir" >/dev/null 2>&1
    
    # Create file with fake AWS key
    echo "AWS_SECRET_KEY=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE" > "$test_dir/config.sh"
    
    log_verbose "Scanning for secrets in config.sh"
    if scan_for_secrets "$test_dir/config.sh"; then
        log_error "FAIL: Should detect AWS key pattern"
        return 1
    fi
    
    rm -rf "$test_dir"
    log_success "gitleaks correctly detected secret"
}

test_secret_scanning_fallback() {
    log_test_start "Secret scanning with heuristic fallback"
    
    local test_dir=$(mktemp -d)
    
    # Test patterns that should trigger heuristic detection
    local secret_patterns=(
        "password=supersecret123"
        "api_key: sk-abc123def456"
        "PRIVATE_KEY-----BEGIN RSA"
        "Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGc..."
    )
    
    for pattern in "${secret_patterns[@]}"; do
        echo "$pattern" > "$test_dir/test.txt"
        log_verbose "Testing heuristic for: ${pattern:0:30}..."
        
        if \! heuristic_secret_scan "$test_dir/test.txt"; then
            # Expected to detect
            log_success "Correctly detected: ${pattern:0:30}..."
        else
            log_error "FAIL: Should detect: ${pattern:0:30}..."
            rm -rf "$test_dir"
            return 1
        fi
    done
    
    rm -rf "$test_dir"
}
```

## Logging Requirements

Every test MUST:
1. Call log_test_start() with test name
2. Use log_verbose() for each check
3. Use log_success() or log_error() for results
4. Report total assertions passed/failed

## Related Beads

- Tests: bd-nqjy (file denylist)
- Tests: bd-0ghe (secret scanning)
- Tests: bd-5iwb (file size/binary)
- Parent epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tests cover all denylist patterns from plan
- [ ] Tests verify size limits at boundary conditions
- [ ] Tests verify binary detection heuristics
- [ ] Tests verify all secret scanning layers (gitleaks, detect-secrets, heuristics)
- [ ] All tests have detailed logging output
- [ ] Tests clean up temp files properly
- [ ] Tests skip gracefully when dependencies missing","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:24:13.162619708-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T17:24:13.162619708-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6p3o","depends_on_id":"bd-nqjy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:37.323508064-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6p3o","depends_on_id":"bd-0ghe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:37.351760361-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6p3o","depends_on_id":"bd-5iwb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:37.373782482-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6p3o","depends_on_id":"bd-2ze9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:38:02.108115191-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6tkb","title":"Implement checkpoint and resume functionality","description":"Checkpoint saves review state for recovery after interrupts.

checkpoint_review_state() saves:
- run_id, mode, timestamp
- repos_total, repos_completed
- pending repos list
- questions_pending

resume_from_checkpoint() restores:
- Load checkpoint file
- Set REVIEW_RUN_ID and mode
- Populate PENDING_REPOS array
- Log count of remaining repos

Checkpoint saved after each repo completes. Resume with --resume flag loads checkpoint and continues. Validates checkpoint is from same config hash.

Acceptance: Checkpoint captures full state, resume continues correctly, handles missing checkpoint gracefully.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:23.906703811-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:32:42.751500361-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T18:32:42.751500361-05:00","close_reason":"Wire resume checkpoint into review discovery","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6tkb","depends_on_id":"bd-z89z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:15.661118806-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -676,7 +676,7 @@ json_is_success() {
 
 # Escape a string for safe embedding in JSON.
 # Usage: escaped=$(json_escape "$raw_string")
-# Handles: backslash, double-quote, newline, tab, carriage return
+# Handles: backslash, double-quote, newline, tab, carriage return, backspace, form feed
 json_escape() {
     local str="${1:-}"
     # Order matters: escape backslashes first, then other chars
@@ -685,6 +685,8 @@ json_escape() {
     str="${str//$'\n'/\\n}"    # newline -> \n
     str="${str//$'\t'/\\t}"    # tab -> \t
     str="${str//$'\r'/\\r}"    # carriage return -> \r
+    str="${str//$'\b'/\\b}"    # backspace -> \b
+    str="${str//$'\f'/\\f}"    # form feed -> \f
     printf '%s' "$str"
 }

-# Wait for Claude Code agent to complete work and return to idle state.
-# Usage: ntm_wait_completion session_name [timeout_seconds]
-# Exit codes:
-#   0 - Agent is idle (condition met)
-#   1 - Timeout exceeded
-#   2 - Error (check error_code in JSON)
-#   3 - Agent error detected via pattern match
-# Output: JSON response on stdout with wait details

 # Get real-time activity state for an ntm session.
 # Usage: ntm_get_activity session_name
 # Output: JSON with agent states, velocity, health, rate_limited flag
@@ -13206,7 +13161,12 @@ run_lint_gate() {
 }
 
 #------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-# run_secret_scan: Scan for secrets in project changes
+# run_secret_scan: Scan for secrets in project changes (bd-0ghe)
+#
+# Uses layered fallback approach:
+#   1. gitleaks (if installed) - comprehensive scanner
+#   2. detect-secrets (if installed) - alternative scanner
+#   3. heuristic regex patterns (fallback) - best effort
 #
 # Args:
 #   $1 - Project directory path
@@ -13235,19 +13195,49 @@ run_secret_scan() {
             gl_summary=$(echo "$gl_output" | head -3 | tr '\n' ' ')
             findings+=("gitleaks: ${gl_summary:-detected potential secrets}")
         fi
+    # Layer 2: Use detect-secrets if available
+    elif command -v detect-secrets &>/dev/null; then
+        log_verbose "Scanning for secrets with detect-secrets"
+        local ds_output ds_count
+        if ds_output=$(detect-secrets scan "$project_dir" 2>/dev/null); then
+            ds_count=$(echo "$ds_output" | jq '.results | length' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+            if [[ "$ds_count" -gt 0 ]]; then
+                exit_code=1
